questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's solar power capacity in 2024 is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERIPHERAL:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Which of the following was a social cost of the Industrial Revolution?
options: Poor working conditions and child labour
Poor working conditions and child labour
questions.solution: Poor working conditions and child labour

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

The Industrial Revolution began in which country?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a risk in underground mining?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Coal grading is primarily based on:

Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's energy demand is expected to grow at ____ % annually.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Gondwana coal fields are located in which part of India?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

What has improved mine safety according to the passage?

Real-time monitoring systems and gas detectors
questions.solution: Real-time monitoring systems and gas detectors

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
options: The sceneries of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains have been breathtaking.
questions.solution: The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Test-driven development (TDD) follows:
options: Red (write failing test) → Green (write code to pass) → Refactor
questions.solution: TDD: write test before code (short development cycle: test-code-refactor).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 8, push 14, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Informational cohesion:
options: A module that performs multiple related operations, each with its own entry point (same data structure)
questions.solution: Informational cohesion: multiple functions operating on same data structure (e.g., a stack with push/pop/isEmpty).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The worst-case complexity of searching in a BST is:
questions.solution: Degenerate BST (skewed) = O(n). Balanced = O(log n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MPLS (Multiprotocol Label Switching):
options: Uses labels instead of IP addresses for fast forwarding (label switching)
questions.solution: MPLS: label = 20-bit + experimental + bottom-of-stack + TTL. LSR (Label Switch Router) swaps labels.

options: A prioritized list of features, bugs, and technical work
questions.solution: Product backlog: dynamic, ordered by value. Sprint backlog: subset for current sprint.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a heap, the largest element is at:
questions.solution: Max-heap: root is maximum element. Min-heap: root is minimum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

options: Path-vector routing protocol used between ASes
questions.solution: BGP = exterior gateway protocol (EGP), path-vector, used between autonomous systems.

options: Individual components or functions in isolation
questions.solution: Unit test = smallest testable part. Integration test = module interactions.

questions.stem: The MX record in DNS specifies:
options: The mail exchange server for a domain
questions.solution: MX: priority + hostname of mail server. Lower priority = preferred.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mutation testing:
options: Introduces small changes (mutants) to code to verify tests can detect them
questions.solution: Mutation testing: good tests kill mutants (detect the change). Mutation score = killed/total.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A max-heap is a complete binary tree in which each parent is:
options: Greater than or equal to its children
questions.solution: In a max-heap every parent ≥ its children, putting the maximum at the root.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: UDP, in contrast to TCP, is:
questions.solution: UDP sends datagrams without setup, acknowledgements or guaranteed delivery.

options: A class has only one instance and provides a global access point

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The height of a binary tree with n nodes (worst case) is:
questions.solution: Skewed tree: height = n-1 (each node has one child).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A gateway operates at:
options: Multiple layers (can translate between different protocols)
questions.solution: Gateway: protocol translator (e.g., email gateway, IoT gateway). Can work at any layer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 10-bit register can represent how many distinct values?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cohesion refers to:
options: The degree to which elements within a module belong together
questions.solution: High cohesion = related functionality grouped. Low cohesion = unrelated functions together.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inserting a node at the front of a singly linked list takes time:
questions.solution: Only a couple of pointer updates are needed — constant time.

questions.stem: Scrum is an agile framework using:
options: Sprints, daily stand-ups, product backlog, and retrospectives
questions.solution: Scrum: sprints (1-4 weeks), standups, sprint review, retrospective.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 9, push 20, pop, push 30. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 30 leaves 30 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A router operates at:
questions.solution: Router: forwards packets based on IP address, maintains routing table (OSPF, BGP, RIP).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Code review:
options: Systematic examination of code by peers to find defects and improve quality
questions.solution: Code review: catches bugs early, enforces standards, shares knowledge (formal or informal).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 11, push 14, pop, push 30. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 30 leaves 30 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fragmentation in IP occurs when:
options: Packet size exceeds MTU of a network link
questions.solution: IP fragmentation: packet split into smaller fragments to fit MTU.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of binary 10110 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Refactoring is:
options: Restructuring existing code without changing its external behavior
questions.solution: Refactoring: improve design, readability, maintainability. Should be backed by tests.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Manacher's algorithm:
options: Finds all palindromic substrings in O(n) time
questions.solution: Manacher: linear time using palindrome radii and symmetry to avoid recomputation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: IPv6 address size is:
questions.solution: IPv6 = 128-bit addresses (8 groups of 16-bit hex). IPv4 = 32-bit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal value of the binary number 101₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Black-box testing:
options: Tests functionality without knowing internal code structure
questions.solution: Black-box: input/output behavior (equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time to check if a binary tree is a BST is:
questions.solution: Inorder traversal of BST = sorted. Check left < root < right recursively.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TCP's congestion control phases are:
options: Slow start, congestion avoidance, fast recovery, fast retransmit
Slow start and fast retransmit only
questions.solution: TCP Tahoe: slow start + congestion avoidance + fast retransmit. Reno: + fast recovery.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal value of the hexadecimal number FF₁₆ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coupling refers to:
options: The number of functions in a module
The degree of interdependence between modules
questions.solution: Low coupling = good (modules independent). High cohesion = good (module focused).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 100×20 array of 8-byte elements occupies how much memory?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Functional Point Analysis (FPA):
options: Measures software size based on functionality (inputs, outputs, queries, files, interfaces)
questions.solution: FPA: external inputs (EI), external outputs (EO), external queries (EQ), internal files (ILF), external interfaces (EIF).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ford-Fulkerson complexity with integer capacities:
questions.solution: Each augmenting path increases flow by at least 1. Integer capacities → finite termination.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The binary number 11111111 in decimal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The FCFS scheduling algorithm can cause:
options: Convoy effect (short processes waiting behind long ones)
questions.solution: Convoy effect: short jobs wait behind long job in FCFS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Entscheidungsproblem (decision problem) asks:
options: Is there an algorithm to determine if a given statement is provable?
questions.solution: Entscheidungsproblem: shown undecidable by Church and Turing (1936).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TSX (Transactional Synchronization Extensions):
options: Hardware transactional memory for optimistic concurrency
questions.solution: TSX: HLE (Hardware Lock Elision) + RTM (Restricted Transactional Memory). Abort on conflict.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of comparisons in selection sort for n=8 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kahn's algorithm for topological sort:
options: Uses in-degree counting: repeatedly remove vertices with in-degree 0
questions.solution: Kahn's: compute in-degrees, queue zeros, remove edges → O(V+E). Produces lexicographic order if priority queue.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: WiFi 802.11ac operates in which band?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A data word of 32 bits occupies how many bytes?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SQL GROUP BY clause is used with:
options: Aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN)
questions.solution: GROUP BY groups rows with same values; used with aggregates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Topological sorting requires:
questions.solution: Topological sort: linear ordering where u before v for every edge u→v. Kahn's algorithm or DFS-based.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of nodes in a complete binary tree of height h=16 (root at 0) is at most:
questions.solution: Max nodes in complete binary tree of height h = 2^(h+1)-1 = 131071.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tarjan's SCC algorithm:
options: Single DFS using low-link values and a stack (finds SCCs in O(V+E))
questions.solution: Tarjan: assigns index + low-link to each node. Nodes with index = low are SCC roots.

questions.stem: Memoization is:
options: Storing results of expensive function calls and reusing when same inputs occur

questions.stem: The average-case time of linear search is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Binary search on a sorted array of 128 elements needs at most how many comparisons?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Latency in a network includes:
options: Propagation, transmission, queuing, and processing delay
questions.solution: Total latency = d_prop + d_trans + d_queue + d_proc.

questions.stem: The boot process loads:
options: The OS kernel into memory (bootloader → kernel)

questions.stem: For the binary search tree shown (root 40), the in-order traversal sequence is:
questions.solution: In-order (left–root–right) of a BST gives ascending order: 7, 15, 20, 40, 60.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The trie data structure is used for:
options: Efficient string search and prefix matching
questions.solution: Trie = prefix tree, O(m) search where m = key length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 5, push 11, push 14, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The DRY principle means:
questions.solution: DRY: every piece of knowledge should have a single, unambiguous representation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The worst-case lookup time of a hash table (all keys colliding) is:
questions.solution: If every key hashes to the same bucket, lookup degrades to scanning a list — O(n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thrashing occurs when:
options: A process spends more time paging than executing
questions.solution: Thrashing = high page fault rate, low CPU utilization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The state minimization of a DFA uses:
options: Minimization by removing unreachable states only
questions.solution: DFA minimization: partition refinement (equivalent states merged).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Session hijacking involves:
options: Stealing a user's session token/cookie to impersonate them
questions.solution: Session hijacking: steal session ID (via XSS, sniffing, fixation). Mitigated by HTTPS + HttpOnly + Secure flags.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Capability Maturity Model (CMM):
options: Assesses the maturity of an organization's software development processes
questions.solution: CMM: developed by SEI (Carnegie Mellon). Five levels of process maturity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cohesion measures:
options: How closely the elements within a module are related
How modules depend on each other
The number of functions in a module
questions.solution: Cohesion (best→worst): functional, sequential, communicational, procedural, temporal, logical, coincidental.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The modulus of a counter is:
options: The number of states the counter cycles through
questions.solution: Mod-n counter: n states (e.g., decade counter = mod-10, 0-9).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A foreign key:
options: References the primary key of another table
questions.solution: Foreign key = column(s) referencing PK of another table (referential integrity).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Linux uses which type of kernel?
questions.solution: Linux: monolithic + loadable kernel modules (LKM) for dynamic loading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A NAND gate is equivalent to:
questions.solution: NAND = NOT(AND). Universal gate (can build any logic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A context-free grammar consists of:
options: V, T, P, S (variables, terminals, productions, start)
questions.solution: CFG = (V, T, P, S) where V = non-terminals, T = terminals, P = rules, S = start.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The offsetof macro:
options: Returns the byte offset of a member within a struct type
Returns the alignment of a member
Returns the address of a member
Returns the size of a member
questions.solution: offsetof(type, member): from <stddef.h>. Useful for manual serialization and container_of.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NoSQL databases are characterized by:
options: Flexible schema, horizontal scaling, and eventual consistency

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The demultiplexer (DEMUX) does:
options: Routes one input to one of many outputs based on select lines
Combines multiple inputs to one output
questions.solution: DEMUX = reverse of MUX: 1 input, 2^n outputs, n selects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Ackermann function:
options: Is computable but not primitive recursive (grows extremely fast)
questions.solution: A(0,n)=n+1, A(m+1,0)=A(m,1), A(m+1,n+1)=A(m,A(m+1,n)). Not primitive recursive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The parity bit is used for:
questions.solution: Parity = single-bit error detection (odd or even parity).
